# Claude Code prompt — [5A] Quote → Edit UX shell — T&C tab + tab-scoped form + Save/Cancel

*Paste below the line. Generated by Cowork, 2026-05-28.*

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow it.

## Background

The Quote → Edit page today shows a tabbed edit form (Cost Summary, Scope items, Provisional Sums, Cost Options, Assumptions, Exclusions, Preview) AND, below the form, the full read-only quote sections (Cost summary table, Assumptions list, Exclusions list, etc.). When the user clicks Edit and switches between sub-tabs, the form area changes but the read-only sections below stay visible, creating visual noise.

Four interrelated UX changes:

1. **Add Terms & Conditions as a sub-tab.** Today T&C clauses can't be edited from the Quote → Edit view. They should live in their own sub-tab and follow the override-highlight + ↺ revert mechanic from PR #239 (clauses that differ from the IS-standard template show with the `--surface-override` tint; clicking ↺ resets to the standard).

2. **Tab-scoped edit form.** When on the Cost Summary sub-tab, only show the Cost Summary form — hide everything else (the read-only sections below shouldn't render). Same for every other sub-tab. The user sees ONLY the section they're editing.

3. **Save / Cancel buttons.** The current `Edit` button toggles into edit mode but the exit affordance is unclear. Change to:
   - When NOT editing: show `Edit` button (current behaviour).
   - When editing: show **Save** + **Cancel** buttons in the same position.
   - **Cancel** discards all unsaved changes (re-fetches from API, reverts local form state, returns to view mode).
   - **Save** persists all changes and returns to view mode.

4. **T&C clause override-highlight.** From PR #239 Part 1: when a T&C clause's text differs from the IS-standard template, the textarea shows with `background: var(--surface-override)` tint and the ↺ revert icon. On revert, the clause resets to the standard template text.

## Phase 0

- `git fetch origin`; branch off `main`: `feat/quote-edit-ux-shell`.
- Conflict-check: `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/QuoteTab.tsx`, `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/ClientQuotesPanel.tsx`, and any related Quote edit components.

## Implementation

### 1. Add T&C sub-tab

In the edit-mode tab list (search for the current sub-tab definitions in `QuoteTab.tsx` or `ClientQuotesPanel.tsx` — look for `"Cost Summary"`, `"Scope items"`, `"Provisional Sums"`, `"Cost Options"`, `"Assumptions"`, `"Exclusions"`, `"Preview"` strings together), insert a new tab **"Terms & Conditions"** between "Exclusions" and "Preview".

The T&C tab content is a list of editable clause cards. Each clause:
- Number (read-only, derived)
- Title (read-only, the IS-standard clause title)
- Body (editable textarea, multi-line)
- Override-highlight: if `currentBody !== standardBody`, wrap the textarea with `OverrideField` (the component from PR #239 with `isOverridden={true}` and `onRevert={() => resetClause(clauseNumber)}`).
- Revert action calls `POST /api/v1/tenders/:tenderId/tandc/reset/:clauseNumber` — this endpoint already exists per the API route map.

The clause list is fetched from `GET /api/v1/tenders/:tenderId/tandc` and updates via `PATCH /api/v1/tenders/:tenderId/tandc` (or per-clause endpoint — use whichever already exists).

### 2. Tab-scoped form rendering

Find where the edit form renders the seven (now eight) sections. Today it likely renders all sections in a single container, with tab-switching just scrolling or showing/hiding individually. Refactor to a `switch (activeTab)` block that renders ONLY the active tab's section. The read-only sections that today appear BELOW the form (Cost summary, Assumptions, Exclusions sections — visible in the second screenshot) should be hidden during edit mode entirely; they're for view-mode only.

Concretely:
- **View mode** (not editing): show the full read-only quote — Cost summary table, Assumptions list, Exclusions list, T&C list, etc. (current behaviour, untouched).
- **Edit mode**: show ONLY the tab list + the active sub-tab's edit form. Nothing else on the page during edit.

### 3. Save / Cancel

Find the current `Edit` button. Replace its render with:

```tsx
{isEditing ? (
  <>
    <button onClick={handleCancel}>Cancel</button>
    <button onClick={handleSave} className="primary">Save</button>
  </>
) : (
  <button onClick={() => setIsEditing(true)}>Edit</button>
)}
```

`handleCancel` discards local form state (re-fetch the quote from API to reset) and exits edit mode.
`handleSave` flushes the local form state to the API (PATCH the quote) and exits edit mode on success. On failure, stay in edit mode and surface the error.

Optimistic UI is fine — but a Cancel after some changes have already been blur-saved (via existing inline save semantics on certain fields) won't be able to undo those. Document this in the PR body: Cancel discards form-level pending state but cannot rewind already-blur-saved field edits. Owner is aware.

### 4. T&C clause override-highlight + reset

Already specified in §1 above. Use the `OverrideField` wrapper component from PR #239. The "is overridden" check is `currentClauseBody.trim() !== standardClauseBody.trim()`. The revert action calls the existing `POST /api/v1/tenders/:tenderId/tandc/reset/:clauseNumber` endpoint.

## Tests

- Component: T&C tab renders; clauses with modified text show tinted; ↺ click calls reset endpoint and reverts the textarea.
- Component: while editing on Cost Summary sub-tab, switching to Scope items shows the Scope items form and hides Cost Summary form; no read-only sections render.
- Component: Save button persists changes via PATCH; Cancel discards local state via re-fetch.
- Integration: full edit cycle — click Edit, modify a clause, modify a cost line, click Save, confirm both persist and view-mode reflects them.
- §6 pre-PR CI checklist green.
